title: Run
description: A single agent run inside a workspace or sandbox. A Run records which spec it targeted, which agent executed it, what inputs were supplied, what events fired, what outputs landed, and what it cost. Runs are the join key between Decisions and Artifacts.

id.description: Stable run identifier, unique within the producing repo.

agent_id.description: Identifier of the executing agent. Example: claude-opus-4-7 or codex-cli.

runtime.description: Runtime environment label. Example: claude-code-cli, codex-cli, github-actions, vercel-build.

workspace_id.description: Identifier of the workspace or sandbox the run executed inside.

started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the run started.

finished_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the run finished. Omitted while the run is still in progress.

inputs.description: Inputs supplied to the run. Each entry is a kind tag plus an opaque ref.

kind.description: Input category. Example: prompt, file, url, dataset.

ref.description: Pointer to the input (path, URL, or id).

events.description: Timeline of notable events during the run.

kind.description: Event type. Example: tool_call, tool_result, gate_check, human_intervention, error.

payload.description: Event-specific payload. Schema varies by kind; consumers must dispatch on kind.

outputs.description: Artifacts the run produced. Each entry references an Artifact by id.

type.description: Optional cached copy of the artifact type for quick scanning.

cost.description: Resource cost of the run. Optional but recommended for any run that calls a paid model.

tokens_input.description: Total input tokens consumed across all model calls in this run.

tokens_output.description: Total output tokens emitted across all model calls in this run.

dollars_estimated.description: Estimated dollar cost of the run, computed from the token counts and the model's posted rates.
